To accelerate enterprise deployment, software heavyweight **ServiceNow** has signed on as the foundational launch partner under a multi-year global agreement, embedding Experian’s underlying data and risk governance frameworks directly into core corporate workflows. The launch marks a transition for enterprise AI within regulated financial markets, shifting the industry focus away from single-prompt generative chatbots and toward networks of autonomous software agents authorized to execute high-stakes lending decisions. ##### **Dismantling the Enterprise AI Bottleneck** Despite massive industry-wide budgets flowing into artificial intelligence research, financial institutions continue to encounter severe operational bottlenecks when attempting to scale autonomous models. The hurdle rarely stems from the algorithms themselves, but rather from the underlying data management systems. New global research published by Experian alongside the launch highlights these structural vulnerabilities: * **Workflow Integration:** Nearly half of global financial organizations (48 per cent) state that integrating complex data layers into automated AI workflows remains heavily problematic. * **Lineage Obscurity:** Approximately one-third of risk executives report poor or unverified data lineage within their legacy models. * **Siloed Infrastructure:** An additional third identify fragmented data silos across isolated internal teams and vendor systems as a primary roadblock to production. Experian’s Agent Operating System is engineered to bypass these roadblocks. It establishes a unified semantic, trust, and orchestration layer that connects software agents developed by Experian, the financial institutions themselves, and third-party technology providers into one controlled operational ecosystem. ##### **Capitalizing on a Shift in Consumer Trust** The urgency for financial firms to deploy production-grade agentic layers is further reinforced by a rapid evolution in consumer comfort with machine autonomy. highlights.content: [Accessibility Menu](#) [▲ S&P 500 **+---%**|▲ Stock Advisor **+---%**Join The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/mms/mark/e-foolcom-sa-top-nav-returns) [Accessibility](#)[Help](https://support.fool.com/) In the race to build ever-larger artificial intelligence (AI) systems, connectivity is quietly emerging as the next critical bottleneck after raw compute and the need for high-bandwidth memory. At the recent Computex conference, **Nvidia** CEO Jensen Huang underscored this shift by publicly endorsing **Marvell Technology** ([MRVL](/quote/nasdaq/mrvl/) +0.21%) -- calling the chipmaker the [next trillion-dollar company](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/10/jensen-huang-fantastic-news-marvell-stock/). Marvell's specialized semiconductors focus on high-speed networking, optical interconnects, and custom silicon. As hyperscalers accelerate data center buildouts, Marvell sits at the intersection of performance, efficiency, and architectural flexibility, positioning it for substantial growth amid the AI infrastructure boom. Image source: The Motley Fool. ## Marvell plays an important role in AI chip stacks Marvell designs the connective tissue that allows artificial intelligence (AI) workloads to operate at scale. The company offers Ethernet switches, optical DSPs for high-bandwidth, low-latency links, and custom application-specific integrated circuits (ASICs). In [hyperscale](https://www.fool.com/terms/h/hyperscalers/) AI data centers, individual GPUs do not operate in isolation. Rather, these accelerators are exchanging enormous volumes of data in real time across tens of thousands of racks and rows. Marvell's solutions address this data flow by delivering the high-speed fabrics, optics, and programmable networking that keep large-scale training clusters and [inference deployments](https://www.fool.com/terms/a/ai-inference/) running efficiently. Marvell's exposure to both critical networking gear and custom silicon makes the company a prime picks-and-shovels player in the AI infrastructure era. By offering a variety of connectivity solutions, the company has multiple avenues for revenue and earnings expansion as AI architectures evolve. ## Analyzing trillion-dollar chip stocks Nvidia's first-mover advantage in GPUs allowed the company to strike early during the initial phases of the generative AI wave. Hence, Nvidia was the first semiconductor stock to enter the [trillion-dollar club](https://www.fool.com/terms/t/trillion-dollar-club/), propelled by its dominant data center services operation. **Broadcom** followed Nvidia's path to a trillion-dollar valuation, riding strong demand for custom AI ASICs and high-speed networking switches that complement GPU clusters. **Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing** later achieved a $1 trillion market cap as investors realized the company's role as an indispensable foundry partner, manufacturing the most advanced nodes for the entire chip ecosystem. Most recently, the [**Micron Technology**](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/06/09/is-marvell-the-next-micron/), **Samsung**, and **SK Hynix** trio joined the exclusive list of trillion-dollar chip stocks as valuations rerate and catch up to price in the potential of the AI memory supercycle. This sequence illustrates how capital has chosen to flow through the AI chip value chain: Investors first rewarded the core compute layer that unlocks new capabilities, then extended these premiums to the enablers of AI deployment at scale. Marvell occupies such an enabling role across high-performance connectivity. ## [NASDAQ: MRVL](/quote/nasdaq/mrvl/) Marvell Technology Today's Change (0.21%) $0.60 Current Price $281.31 ### Key Data Points Market Cap $245B Day's Range $267.50- $287.97 52wk Range $61.44- $324.20 Volume 1.8M Avg Vol 34.6M Gross Margin 50.64% Dividend Yield 0.09% ## How can Marvell reach a $1 trillion market cap? highlights.content: [Skip to content](#content) [AI Investor Podcast](https://podcast.247wallst.com/?utm_source=247wallst&utm_campaign=site_topnav) [S&P 500](https://247wallst.com/indices/sp-500/) [Dow Jones](https://247wallst.com/indices/dow-jones/) [Nasdaq 100](https://247wallst.com/indices/nasdaq-100/) [Russell 2000](https://247wallst.com/indices/russell-2000/) [FTSE 100 10,463.20 +0.53%](https://247wallst.com/indices/ftse-100/) [Nikkei 225 67,337.50 +1.40%](https://247wallst.com/indices/nikkei-225/) [Investing](https://247wallst.com/./investing/) # 3 Beaten Down AI Infrastructure Stocks to Buy in June By [Joel South](https://247wallst.com/author/247joel/) Published ### Quick Read * NVDA trades 26% below its 52-week high despite 85% YoY revenue growth, while AVGO dropped 22% in one week on a sell-the-news overreaction. * MSFT's AI business surpassed a $37 billion annual run rate, up 123% YoY, yet carries the group's lowest forward P/E at 21. * **Act now:** the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Broadcom didn't make the cut. [Grab the names FREE today](https://247wallst.com/lp/top-10-ai-stocks/AVGO/?i=35c2fd31-a09d-4dbf-99ac-670cf09b53ac&p=aded526a-f78d-426a-9c96-82c54af7e0ab&pos=keypoints&tpid=1609273). ​ ### How to Add Us to Google News Sending You to Google News in 3 [AI infrastructure stocks](https://247wallst.com/investing/2025/09/02/5-ai-stocks-executives-are-accumulating/ "5 AI Stocks Executives Are Accumulating") have given investors a textbook entry window this month. The trio that anchors the spending cycle has all pulled back from spring highs, even as the underlying revenue trajectory keeps accelerating. **NVIDIA** **([NASDAQ:NVDA](https://247wallst.com/companies/NVDA/) | [NVDA Price Prediction](https://247wallst.com/companies/nvda/price-prediction))**, **Broadcom** **([NASDAQ:AVGO](https://247wallst.com/companies/AVGO/))** and **Microsoft** **([NASDAQ:MSFT](https://247wallst.com/companies/MSFT/))** are the picks-and-shovels triangle of the AI buildout: silicon, custom accelerators and networking, and the hyperscale cloud platform monetizing it. All three are trading meaningfully below their 52-week highs heading into mid-June. ## NVIDIA (NVDA) NVIDIA closed at $200.42 on June 10, down 7% over the past week and 9% over the past month. That puts the stock 26% below its 52-week high of $236.26, even though the year-to-date return remains positive at 8%. The bull case rests on a fundamental engine that is still compounding. Q1 FY2027 revenue hit $81.61 billion, up 85% year over year, with [Data Center revenue](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/04/30/ai-data-center-spending-rushes-toward-800-billion/ "AI Data Center Spending Rushes Toward $800 Billion") alone at $75.25 billion (+92% YoY) and Data Center Networking up 199% YoY to $14.8 billion. Management guided Q2 to $91.0 billion in revenue at a 75% non-GAAP gross margin. CEO Jensen Huang framed the moment as “the largest infrastructure expansion in human history.” The board paired the growth story with capital return: a quarterly dividend bumped from $0.01 to $0.25 and an additional $80 billion buyback authorization. Forward P/E sits at 23 against an analyst consensus target of $298.42, with 95% bullish analyst sentiment. **Risk:** Q2 guidance assumes no H20 Data Center compute revenue from China due to export restrictions, and a beta of 2.2 means the next macro shock cuts deeper here than in the broader market. ## Broadcom (AVGO) Broadcom is the sharpest dip in the group. Shares finished at $372.10, down 22% in a single week and 5% on June 10 alone. The 52-week high is $495. The catalyst was a classic sell-the-news reaction to a strong report: Seeking Alpha noted that “Broadcom reported record Q2 results with significant revenue and AI semiconductor growth, but its stock dropped nearly 15% due to guidance failing to meet elevated investor expectations and a declining gross margin outlook.” That reaction looks like an overcorrection against the ...

Now they’re turning ‘welcomer cities’ into the next big tech towns](https://fortune.com/article/why-is-gen-z-moving-to-nashville-orlando-next-big-tech-towns/?itm_source=parsely-api) # 2 [Social Security's 2032 deadline puts a 22% cut on the table — but Washington has way less room to negotiate than 1983](https://fortune.com/2026/06/12/social-security-2032-deadline-benefit-cut-congress/?itm_source=parsely-api) # 3 [Trump expects to sign a deal with Iran on Sunday, but Tehran may want to avoid giving him a gift on his birthday](https://fortune.com/2026/06/13/trump-us-iran-peace-deal-mou-birthday-gift/?itm_source=parsely-api) [AI](/section/artificial-intelligence/)[Nvidia](/tag/nvidia/) # ‘The cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employee’: Nvidia executive says right now AI is more expensive than paying human workers By [Sasha Rogelberg](/author/sasha-rogelberg/) Sasha Rogelberg Reporter By [Sasha Rogelberg](/author/sasha-rogelberg/) Sasha Rogelberg Reporter June 14, 2026, 10:49 AM ET Nvidia vice president of applied deep learning Bryan Catanzaro said right now, AI costs more than employees.Big Event Media—Getty Images for HumanX Conference [Recent tech layoffs](https://fortune.com/2026/04/23/meta-microsoft-layoffs-job-cuts-not-filling-open-roles-voluntary-buyouts/) would initially appear to indicate the great labor shift from human workers to AI may already be happening. Recommended Video --- [Meta](https://fortune.com/company/facebook/) announced in a memo earlier this year it plans to lay off 10% of its workforce, about 8,000 employees, as well as scrap plans to hire for 6,000 open positions. It’s part of an effort to “run the company more efficiently and to allow us to offset the other investments we’re making,” according to the memo. [Microsoft](https://fortune.com/company/microsoft/) has offered thousands of its own employees a voluntary buyout, the largest the company has ever offered. Other tech headers, however, suggest that right now, AI isn’t saving companies money on labor; it’s actually costing them more than the humans they currently employ. “For my team, the cost of compute is far beyond the costs of the employees,” Bryan Catanzaro, vice president of applied deep learning at [Nvidia](https://fortune.com/company/nvidia/), [told *Axios*](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/26/ai-cost-human-workers) in April. An MIT [study](https://www.csail.mit.edu/news/rethinking-ais-impact-mit-csail-study-reveals-economic-limits-job-automation) from 2024 backs up these executives’ experiences. Analyzing the technical requirements of AI models needed to perform jobs at a human level, researchers found that AI automation would be economically viable in only 23% of roles where vision is a primary part of the work. In the remaining 77% of the time, it was cheaper for humans to continue their work. In other instances, AI has proved to be fallible, with one engineer saying an AI agent [destroyed his database](https://fortune.com/2026/03/18/ai-coding-risks-amazon-agents-enterprise/) and network as a result of what he called “overuse.” ## Why are tech firms pouring money into AI despite financial pressures? highlights.content: [AI](https://wccftech.com/topic/ai/) [Hardware](https://wccftech.com/topic/hardware/) # NVIDIA GB300 Dominates Agentic AI Workloads With 20x Performance Leap Over Hopper As Rubin Nears Launch [Hassan Mujtaba](https://wccftech.com/author/hms/) • [Add Wccftech on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=wccftech.com) [Comments](#comments "View comments") NVIDIA's [Blackwell GB300](https://wccftech.com/nvidias-blackwell-ultra-pushes-agentic-ai-performance-to-new-heights/) has posted record performance in AA-AgentPerf, a new benchmark that measures Agentic AI workflows. ## NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GB300 is 20 Times Faster Than Hopper In Agentic AI, Records Highest Performance In Latest Benchmarks Artificial Analysis has a new benchmark out called [AA-AgentPerf](https://artificialanalysis.ai/methodology/agentperf), which measures how many active agents an inference deployment can support under realistic workloads, which include: [##### **Related Story** NVIDIA’s First Co-Packaged Optics Switch Lands at Lambda, Cutting 3kW Per Rack and Freeing Power for 3,137 Extra GPUs](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-first-co-packaged-optics-switch-lands-at-lambda-cutting-3kw-per-rack/) * **Real agentic trajectories** — multi-turn coding sessions with interleaved reasoning, tool calls, and variable context lengths (not synthetic uniform prompts). * **Sustained concurrent load** — simulated agents maintain continuous in-flight requests, stressing KV cache reuse, speculative decoding, and scheduler behavior. * **Market-derived SLO tiers** — performance thresholds based on Artificial Analysis serverless API benchmarking data, reflecting quality-of-service levels observed across providers. * **Continuously updated** — results are updated on an ongoing basis as new hardware, software stacks, and model versions become available. * **Production-ready** — models are tested with realistic optimizations enabled and production-scale deployment topologies. The AA-AgentPerf benchmark is used to measure three key metrics, which form the basis of modern-day AI deployments, such as: * **Time to First Token (TTFT):** Per-request latency from sending the request to receiving the first output token. * **Output Speed:** Per-request output tokens per second, measured after the first token is received. * **System Output Throughput:** Aggregate output tokens per second across all concurrent agents. NVIDIA is now [publishing its first benchmarks](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-achieves-leading-agentic-coding-performance-on-first-agentic-ai-benchmark/) in AgentPerf measures using DeepSeek V4 Pro on its GB300 NVL72 platform. This model represents the type of Frontier models that power agents today & are widely used for AI. In the first round of benchmarks, NVIDIA has recorded the fastest performance with its GB300 hardware, posting a 20x lead (per MegaWatt) over its older HGX H200 platform. GB300 can sustain up to 60,000 concurrent agents per MW, a massive leap over Hopper. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Benchmark** | **Value of metric** | **NVIDIA GB300****NVL72** | **NVIDIA H200** | | Concurrent agents per MW | **Energy efficiency**: How many active agents a system can support for a given power budget | 61.4K | 2.6K | | Concurrent agents per GPU | **Hardware efficiency**: How much serving capacity is achieved per GPU | 57.5 | 1.4 | NVIDIA states that the performance highlights NVIIA's GB300 NVL72 and Blackwell's ability to run large-scale agentic coding workloads while keeping the GPUs fully utilized across several concurrent agent sessions. Looking forward, [NVIDIA's Rubin](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-vera-rubin-enters-full-production-ready-to-power-agentic-ai-factories/) is just on the horizon and is expected to extend these leads through a supercharged AI architecture, which will offer 50 PFLOPs of compute from NVFP4, and with the Vera CPU, the LLM tool calls and end-to-end performance will see major performance and efficiency gains. **About the [a...

highlights.title: Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again - Qoo Media
highlights.titleZh: Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again - Qoo Media
highlights.content: [Random](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/gadget/49505/vivo-x300-series-launches-as-the-new-king-of-mobile-camera-technology/) * [Tech](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tech/) * [Gadget](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/gadget/) * [Lifestyle](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/lifestyle/) * [International](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/international/) # Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again Author: [Qoo Media](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/author/qoomedia/) [GAdd on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=qoo10.co.id) [Anthropic](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/anthropic/) has opened access to [Claude Fable 5](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/claude-fable-5/) for a limited period, giving more paid users a chance to try one of its most capable AI models without paying extra. The move brings a model with [Mythos-grade intelligence](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/mythos-grade-ai/) into everyday use, but only temporarily. The company is positioning Fable 5 as a general-use model built from the same advanced foundation as Claude Mythos. That matters because Mythos itself was previously kept away from ordinary users due to the risks tied to its security-focused capabilities. **Temporary free access for paid plans** For now, Anthropic is including Claude Fable 5 at no additional cost for Pro, Max, Team, and seat-based Enterprise subscribers. The offer is limited, and the clock is already set. Access remains free only until 23 June. After that, continued use of Fable 5 will require usage credits, unless Anthropic later decides to restore it as a no-cost benefit inside paid plans. That makes the rollout less like a permanent upgrade and more like a time-limited test of how widely the model can be used. Users get an early look at Anthropic’s most intelligent consumer-facing option, but the current setup will not stay free forever. **Why Mythos was kept restricted** Claude Mythos was known as a large language model built for highly sensitive security work rather than broad public use. Anthropic viewed wide access as too risky, even though the model was described as stronger than other models in functional terms. Fable 5 is meant to open much of that power to a wider audience while leaving out the most sensitive parts. Anthropic’s goal is to give users the benefit of its smartest model without exposing capabilities that could be misused. **Built-in safety measures remain in place** Anthropic says Fable 5 still includes safeguards for risky topics. When the system detects a query that falls into those categories, the request is routed to another model instead. The fallback model is Claude Opus 4.8, which Anthropic describes as its next most capable model. The company says this arrangement helps it launch faster while keeping safety controls active from day one. Those safeguards are intentionally conservative, which means some harmless requests may also be redirected. Even so, Anthropic says the trigger happens in less than 5% of sessions on average, suggesting the interruption should remain limited for most users. The company is also acknowledging a broader risk: even without a security-silo focus, a powerful model like Fable 5 could still be tested by bad actors, including for malware-related development. That is part of why the guardrails are built into the rollout. **What the launch says about the AI market** The release reflects how quickly the AI industry is moving toward broader access to more powerful systems. Models that were once considered too sensitive for general users are increasingly being adapted for everyday products. For Anthropic, Fable 5 is a balancing act between capability and restraint. It expands access to the company’s smartest AI while keeping a clear line around the areas that carry the most risk. For subscribers on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, the immediate benefit is obvious during the promotional window. They can use Mythos-grade intelligence without paying beyond their existing s...

June 14, 2026 • [Dan Lohrmann](https://www.govtech.com/authors/dan-lohrmann.html) * [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/dialog/share?app_id=314190606794339&display=popup&href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026) * [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026&mini=true&title=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026&summary=A%20leadership%20guide%20to%20trust%2C%20governance%20and%20workforce%20evolution%20in%20a%20rapidly%20shifting%20technology%20landscape.&source=GovTech) * [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026&text=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026) * [Print](javascript:window.print()) * [Email](mailto:?body=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026%0A%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026%0A%0AA%20leadership%20guide%20to%20trust%2C%20governance%20and%20workforce%20evolution%20in%20a%20rapidly%20shifting%20technology%20landscape.) What strategies are global organizations using to transform business processes in the era of agentic AI? Where is the new tactical boundary between innovation and risk? How are leaders managing staff differently now, as AI tools enhance efficiency? Who can we emulate to try and keep up with radical change? When will this craziness end — if ever? These are just a few of the questions I have been asked over the past few months as I travel around the country when talking with CISOs, CIOs, CTOs, CFOs and others in government leadership. ### ADDRESSING GOVERNMENT RISK IN 2026 As we head into the second half of the year, global trends in cybersecurity are changing rapidly as artificial intelligence tools take center stage. We are witnessing unprecedented transformations to public- and private-sector organizations as people, process and technology changes become the new normal. For more background, just in the past month, some of the top cyber themes that this blog has covered include pieces of these themes: * [The Mythos Race: Trump’s New EO and Glasswing’s Expansion](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/the-mythos-race-trumps-new-eo-and-glasswings-expansion) * [No Longer Invisible: When Cyber Attacks Go Physical](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/no-longer-invisible-when-cyber-attacks-go-physical) * [How New College Grads Can Succeed in an AI Economy](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/how-new-college-grads-can-succeed-in-an-ai-economy) * [Protecting People and Infrastructure: A 2026 World Cup Security Preview](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/protecting-people-and-infrastructure-a-2026-world-cup-security-preview) In order to examine a few of these topics and questions from a wider, fresh perspective, I try and take a big step back at least once a year to examine what is being published about IT strategies internationally. No doubt, these best practices may not apply in your situation. Nevertheless, my hope is that these examples will motivate and encourage risk mitigation in innovative ways. First, a new report that came from [Global Government Forum in Australia](https://www.globalgovernmentforum.com/people-skills-partners-ggf-study-outlines-the-foundations-for-future-ready-government-digital-capability/) that explores how government organizatio...

highlights.title: AI Timekeeping Platforms - Trend Hunter
highlights.content: # AI Timekeeping Platforms [View More](/tech) ## Billables AI Has Raised Series A Funding Led By Avenue Growth Partners [AICE Agent](/AICEAgent) — Jun 14, 2026 — [Tech](/tech) References: [law](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2026/06/09/timekeeping-startup-billables-ai-announces-102m-series-a/) & <billables.ai> Billables AI, a San Francisco startup, launched an AI-powered timekeeping and task-intelligence platform for legal teams, featuring API-driven data capture designed to avoid screen-monitoring and improve accuracy and privacy. The company announced a roughly $10.2 million Series A led by Avenue Growth Partners to scale product development and go-to-market efforts. Founded in 2024, Billables AI integrates with law firm systems to capture activity data and support AI-assisted timekeeping and operational insights. The funding will support hiring across engineering, product and customer success, as well as continued R&D to improve platform accuracy and expand capabilities beyond core timekeeping workflows. Serving more than 200 law firms, particularly midmarket firms, Billables AI aims to turn operational data into tools for pricing and cost analysis, staffing insights and AI governance. For legal buyers, the platform promises more accurate time capture, greater operational visibility and efficiency gains as firms move away from manual time-entry processes toward data-driven workflows. ## Trend Themes 1. **AI-assisted Timekeeping** — Legal teams gain more accurate billable-hour capture through platforms that infer work activity from integrated business systems without relying on invasive screen monitoring. 2. **Privacy-first Productivity Analytics** — API-based activity intelligence creates room for workplace software that balances operational visibility with stronger employee privacy and data governance standards. 3. **Legal Operations Intelligence** — Operational data from daily legal workflows is becoming a foundation for pricing analysis, staffing decisions and profitability insights across midmarket firms. ## Industry Implications 1. **Legal Technology** — AI-native platforms are reshaping law firm infrastructure by replacing manual administrative processes with automated, data-driven workflow intelligence. 2. **Professional Services** — Billable-work environments benefit from task intelligence systems that connect time capture, cost analysis and resource planning across client-facing teams. 3. **Enterprise Software** — Integrated productivity tools are expanding beyond workflow automation into governance-ready analytics that support compliance, accuracy and privacy-sensitive adoption. highlights.content: [AI](https://wccftech.com/topic/ai/) [Hardware](https://wccftech.com/topic/hardware/) # NVIDIA GB300 Dominates Agentic AI Workloads With 20x Performance Leap Over Hopper As Rubin Nears Launch [Hassan Mujtaba](https://wccftech.com/author/hms/) • [Add Wccftech on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=wccftech.com) [Comments](#comments "View comments") NVIDIA's [Blackwell GB300](https://wccftech.com/nvidias-blackwell-ultra-pushes-agentic-ai-performance-to-new-heights/) has posted record performance in AA-AgentPerf, a new benchmark that measures Agentic AI workflows. ## NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GB300 is 20 Times Faster Than Hopper In Agentic AI, Records Highest Performance In Latest Benchmarks Artificial Analysis has a new benchmark out called [AA-AgentPerf](https://artificialanalysis.ai/methodology/agentperf), which measures how many active agents an inference deployment can support under realistic workloads, which include: [##### **Related Story** NVIDIA’s First Co-Packaged Optics Switch Lands at Lambda, Cutting 3kW Per Rack and Freeing Power for 3,137 Extra GPUs](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-first-co-packaged-optics-switch-lands-at-lambda-cutting-3kw-per-rack/) * **Real agentic trajectories** — multi-turn coding sessions with interleaved reasoning, tool calls, and variable context lengths (not synthetic uniform prompts). * **Sustained concurrent load** — simulated agents maintain continuous in-flight requests, stressing KV cache reuse, speculative decoding, and scheduler behavior. * **Market-derived SLO tiers** — performance thresholds based on Artificial Analysis serverless API benchmarking data, reflecting quality-of-service levels observed across providers. * **Continuously updated** — results are updated on an ongoing basis as new hardware, software stacks, and model versions become available. * **Production-ready** — models are tested with realistic optimizations enabled and production-scale deployment topologies. The AA-AgentPerf benchmark is used to measure three key metrics, which form the basis of modern-day AI deployments, such as: * **Time to First Token (TTFT):** Per-request latency from sending the request to receiving the first output token. * **Output Speed:** Per-request output tokens per second, measured after the first token is received. * **System Output Throughput:** Aggregate output tokens per second across all concurrent agents. NVIDIA is now [publishing its first benchmarks](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-achieves-leading-agentic-coding-performance-on-first-agentic-ai-benchmark/) in AgentPerf measures using DeepSeek V4 Pro on its GB300 NVL72 platform. This model represents the type of Frontier models that power agents today & are widely used for AI. In the first round of benchmarks, NVIDIA has recorded the fastest performance with its GB300 hardware, posting a 20x lead (per MegaWatt) over its older HGX H200 platform. GB300 can sustain up to 60,000 concurrent agents per MW, a massive leap over Hopper. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Benchmark** | **Value of metric** | **NVIDIA GB300****NVL72** | **NVIDIA H200** | | Concurrent agents per MW | **Energy efficiency**: How many active agents a system can support for a given power budget | 61.4K | 2.6K | | Concurrent agents per GPU | **Hardware efficiency**: How much serving capacity is achieved per GPU | 57.5 | 1.4 | NVIDIA states that the performance highlights NVIIA's GB300 NVL72 and Blackwell's ability to run large-scale agentic coding workloads while keeping the GPUs fully utilized across several concurrent agent sessions. Looking forward, [NVIDIA's Rubin](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-vera-rubin-enters-full-production-ready-to-power-agentic-ai-factories/) is just on the horizon and is expected to extend these leads through a supercharged AI architecture, which will offer 50 PFLOPs of compute from NVFP4, and with the Vera CPU, the LLM tool calls and end-to-end performance will see major performance and efficiency gains. **About the [a...

AI Agents.content: [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/thefintechtimes "Facebook")[Twitter](https://twitter.com/thefintechtimes "Twitter")[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/the_fintech_times/ "Instagram")[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/10067847/ "Linkedin")[Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCV68tm1NgG-PZzgRmCK5gBA/ "Youtube") * [About](https://thefintechtimes.com/about/) * [Editorial Calendar](https://thefintechtimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/2026-Editorial-Calendar-Jan-Dec.pdf) * [Contact Us](https://thefintechtimes.com/contact-us/) * [Advertise](https://thefintechtimes.com/mediapack/) * [Jobs](https://jobs.thefintechtimes.com/) * [Home](https://thefintechtimes.com) * [World-Region-Country](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/) * [Europe](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/europe/) * [Experian and ServiceNow Launch ‘Agent Operating System’ to Bring Regulated Agentic AI to Lending Workflows](https://thefintechtimes.com/experian-and-servicenow-launch-agent-operating-system-to-bring-regulated-agentic-ai-to-lending-workflows/) [AI](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/news/ai/) [Europe](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/europe/) # Experian and ServiceNow Launch ‘Agent Operating System’ to Bring Regulated Agentic AI to Lending Workflows by [The Fintech Times](https://thefintechtimes.com/author/the-fintech-times/) #### *In one of the most significant artificial intelligence infrastructure rollouts at this year’s Money20/20 Europe conference, global information services giant **Experian** has launched the Experian Agent Operating System™ (AOS).* Unveiled on the Horizon Stage in Amsterdam, the platform arrives as a new core capability within Experian’s established Ascend Platform. To accelerate enterprise deployment, software heavyweight **ServiceNow** has signed on as the foundational launch partner under a multi-year global agreement, embedding Experian’s underlying data and risk governance frameworks directly into core corporate workflows. The launch marks a transition for enterprise AI within regulated financial markets, shifting the industry focus away from single-prompt generative chatbots and toward networks of autonomous software agents authorized to execute high-stakes lending decisions. ##### **Dismantling the Enterprise AI Bottleneck** Despite massive industry-wide budgets flowing into artificial intelligence research, financial institutions continue to encounter severe operational bottlenecks when attempting to scale autonomous models. The hurdle rarely stems from the algorithms themselves, but rather from the underlying data management systems. New global research published by Experian alongside the launch highlights these structural vulnerabilities: * **Workflow Integration:** Nearly half of global financial organizations (48 per cent) state that integrating complex data layers into automated AI workflows remains heavily problematic. * **Lineage Obscurity:** Approximately one-third of risk executives report poor or unverified data lineage within their legacy models. * **Siloed Infrastructure:** An additional third identify fragmented data silos across isolated internal teams and vendor systems as a primary roadblock to production. Experian’s Agent Operating System is engineered to bypass these roadblocks. It establishes a unified semantic, trust, and orchestration layer that connects software agents developed by Experian, the financial institutions themselves, and third-party technology providers into one controlled operational ecosystem. ##### **Capitalizing on a Shift in Consumer Trust** The urgency for financial firms to deploy production-grade agentic layers is further reinforced by a rapid evolution in consumer comfort with machine autonomy. A **HarrisX** poll conducted between March and April 2026 revealed that 55 per cent of global consumers are now willing to authorize an AI agent to execute autonomous commercial purchases on their beh...

[Lohrmann on Cybersecurity](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity) # The Global State of Technology Risk in 2026 ## A leadership guide to trust, governance and workforce evolution in a rapidly shifting technology landscape. June 14, 2026 • [Dan Lohrmann](https://www.govtech.com/authors/dan-lohrmann.html) * [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/dialog/share?app_id=314190606794339&display=popup&href=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026) * [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026&mini=true&title=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026&summary=A%20leadership%20guide%20to%20trust%2C%20governance%20and%20workforce%20evolution%20in%20a%20rapidly%20shifting%20technology%20landscape.&source=GovTech) * [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026&text=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026) * [Print](javascript:window.print()) * [Email](mailto:?body=The%20Global%20State%20of%20Technology%20Risk%20in%202026%0A%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.govtech.com%2Fblogs%2Flohrmann-on-cybersecurity%2Fthe-global-state-of-technology-risk-in-2026%0A%0AA%20leadership%20guide%20to%20trust%2C%20governance%20and%20workforce%20evolution%20in%20a%20rapidly%20shifting%20technology%20landscape.) What strategies are global organizations using to transform business processes in the era of agentic AI? Where is the new tactical boundary between innovation and risk? How are leaders managing staff differently now, as AI tools enhance efficiency? Who can we emulate to try and keep up with radical change? When will this craziness end — if ever? These are just a few of the questions I have been asked over the past few months as I travel around the country when talking with CISOs, CIOs, CTOs, CFOs and others in government leadership. ### ADDRESSING GOVERNMENT RISK IN 2026 As we head into the second half of the year, global trends in cybersecurity are changing rapidly as artificial intelligence tools take center stage. We are witnessing unprecedented transformations to public- and private-sector organizations as people, process and technology changes become the new normal. For more background, just in the past month, some of the top cyber themes that this blog has covered include pieces of these themes: * [The Mythos Race: Trump’s New EO and Glasswing’s Expansion](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/the-mythos-race-trumps-new-eo-and-glasswings-expansion) * [No Longer Invisible: When Cyber Attacks Go Physical](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/no-longer-invisible-when-cyber-attacks-go-physical) * [How New College Grads Can Succeed in an AI Economy](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/how-new-college-grads-can-succeed-in-an-ai-economy) * [Protecting People and Infrastructure: A 2026 World Cup Security Preview](https://www.govtech.com/blogs/lohrmann-on-cybersecurity/protecting-people-and-infrastructure-a-2026-world-cup-security-preview) In order to examine a few of these topics and questions from a wider, fresh perspective, I try and take a big step back at least once a year to examine what is being published about IT strategies internationally. No doubt, these best practices may not apply in your situation. Nevertheless, my hope is that these examples will motivate and encourage risk mitigation in innovative ways. First, a new report that came from [Global Government Forum in Australia](https://www.globalgovernmentforum.com/people-skills-partners-ggf-study-outlines-the-foundations-for-future-ready-government-digital-capability/) that explores how government organizatio...

AI Agents.content: # AI Timekeeping Platforms [View More](/tech) ## Billables AI Has Raised Series A Funding Led By Avenue Growth Partners [AICE Agent](/AICEAgent) — Jun 14, 2026 — [Tech](/tech) References: [law](https://www.law.com/legaltechnews/2026/06/09/timekeeping-startup-billables-ai-announces-102m-series-a/) & <billables.ai> Billables AI, a San Francisco startup, launched an AI-powered timekeeping and task-intelligence platform for legal teams, featuring API-driven data capture designed to avoid screen-monitoring and improve accuracy and privacy. The company announced a roughly $10.2 million Series A led by Avenue Growth Partners to scale product development and go-to-market efforts. Founded in 2024, Billables AI integrates with law firm systems to capture activity data and support AI-assisted timekeeping and operational insights. The funding will support hiring across engineering, product and customer success, as well as continued R&D to improve platform accuracy and expand capabilities beyond core timekeeping workflows. Serving more than 200 law firms, particularly midmarket firms, Billables AI aims to turn operational data into tools for pricing and cost analysis, staffing insights and AI governance. For legal buyers, the platform promises more accurate time capture, greater operational visibility and efficiency gains as firms move away from manual time-entry processes toward data-driven workflows. ## Trend Themes 1. **AI-assisted Timekeeping** — Legal teams gain more accurate billable-hour capture through platforms that infer work activity from integrated business systems without relying on invasive screen monitoring. 2. **Privacy-first Productivity Analytics** — API-based activity intelligence creates room for workplace software that balances operational visibility with stronger employee privacy and data governance standards. 3. **Legal Operations Intelligence** — Operational data from daily legal workflows is becoming a foundation for pricing analysis, staffing decisions and profitability insights across midmarket firms. ## Industry Implications 1. **Legal Technology** — AI-native platforms are reshaping law firm infrastructure by replacing manual administrative processes with automated, data-driven workflow intelligence. 2. **Professional Services** — Billable-work environments benefit from task intelligence systems that connect time capture, cost analysis and resource planning across client-facing teams. 3. **Enterprise Software** — Integrated productivity tools are expanding beyond workflow automation into governance-ready analytics that support compliance, accuracy and privacy-sensitive adoption. AI Agents.titleZh: Banks Confront AI Errors, Oversight and Security - Let's Data Science
AI Agents.content: [Skip to content](#main-content) [LDS Let's Data ScienceLEARN • BUILD • STAY AHEAD](/) [Sign in](/login?redirect=%2Fnews%2Fbanks-confront-ai-errors-oversight-and-security-617918c4) Security & Riskbankingfinancial servicesai riskcybersecurity # Banks Confront AI Errors, Oversight and Security | 4.5 Relevance Score According to Forbes, John Werner reports that AI in finance delivers speed and insight while raising concerns about errors, accountability, cybersecurity, and oversight. Forbes covers a panel at the Imagination in Action conference in Boston in April featuring Celestino Amore of IlliquidX.AI, Miquel Noguer of the Artificial Intelligence Finance Institute, and Brian Peltonen of Parcosm AI. Panel discussion points included how accountability for AI-caused harm flows through the chain of developers, companies, and executives who shaped and deployed the system; how governments are building rules for high-risk AI uses requiring transparency and documentation; and how accountability is distributed across insurers, executives, and builders as AI autonomy grows. Amore was quoted by Forbes on the importance of containment controls. ### What happened According to **Forbes**, John Werner reports that AI adoption in banking improves speed and insight but increases the risk of errors, accountability gaps, and cybersecurity exposure. **Forbes** covers a panel at the Imagination in Action conference in Boston in April featuring **Celestino Amore** of **IlliquidX.AI**, **Miquel Noguer** of the **Artificial Intelligence Finance Institute**, and **Brian Peltonen** of **Parcosm AI**. Werner reports that panel discussion touched on how accountability for AI-caused harm flows through the chain of developers, companies, managers, and leaders who shaped and released the system; how governments are building rules for high-risk AI uses requiring transparency, oversight, and clear documentation; and how accountability is distributed across insurers, executives, and builders as AI autonomy grows. Forbes also quotes Amore on the importance of containment controls for their AI systems. ### Technical context Large language models and other neural systems used in finance are subject to well-known failure modes such as hallucination, data drift, and brittle out-of-distribution behaviour. Companies and practitioners deploying these models typically rely on layered controls - input validation, verification datasets, human-in-the-loop checks, and red-team testing - to reduce operational errors. Observers following the sector note that auditability and reproducible evaluation pipelines materially affect a model's operational risk profile. ### Industry context Reporting places this coverage inside a broader regulatory push: governments and regulators worldwide are increasingly treating high-risk financial AI as a governed category that requires documented oversight. Industry reporting frames accountability as distributed along the supply chain, meaning insurers, vendors, integrators, and financial institutions can all carry exposure when automated decisions cause harm. For practitioners, this translates into tighter expectations for provenance, model lineage, and accessible evidence of testing. ### What to watch Indicators to monitor include regulatory guidance that defines "high-risk" financial use cases, disclosure and documentation standards from supervisory bodies, and vendor-level attestations for security and containment. Industry observers will also track whether banks and service providers publish standardized evaluation benchmarks or third-party audit results for production models. ## Scoring Rationale Single Forbes contributor article summarizing an April conference panel discussion on AI risk in banking. The panelists and institutions are real but the article represents soft editorial coverage of a 2-month-old event, not a regulatory action, product release, or research landmark. LLMs.title: Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again - Qoo Media
LLMs.titleZh: Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again - Qoo Media
LLMs.content: [Random](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/gadget/49505/vivo-x300-series-launches-as-the-new-king-of-mobile-camera-technology/) * [Tech](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tech/) * [Gadget](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/gadget/) * [Lifestyle](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/lifestyle/) * [International](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/international/) # Claude Fable 5 Opens to More Users, but Only Until Anthropic Closes the Door Again Author: [Qoo Media](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/author/qoomedia/) [GAdd on Google](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=qoo10.co.id) [Anthropic](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/anthropic/) has opened access to [Claude Fable 5](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/claude-fable-5/) for a limited period, giving more paid users a chance to try one of its most capable AI models without paying extra. The move brings a model with [Mythos-grade intelligence](https://www.qoo10.co.id/en/tag/mythos-grade-ai/) into everyday use, but only temporarily. The company is positioning Fable 5 as a general-use model built from the same advanced foundation as Claude Mythos. That matters because Mythos itself was previously kept away from ordinary users due to the risks tied to its security-focused capabilities. **Temporary free access for paid plans** For now, Anthropic is including Claude Fable 5 at no additional cost for Pro, Max, Team, and seat-based Enterprise subscribers. The offer is limited, and the clock is already set. Access remains free only until 23 June. After that, continued use of Fable 5 will require usage credits, unless Anthropic later decides to restore it as a no-cost benefit inside paid plans. That makes the rollout less like a permanent upgrade and more like a time-limited test of how widely the model can be used. Users get an early look at Anthropic’s most intelligent consumer-facing option, but the current setup will not stay free forever. **Why Mythos was kept restricted** Claude Mythos was known as a large language model built for highly sensitive security work rather than broad public use. Anthropic viewed wide access as too risky, even though the model was described as stronger than other models in functional terms. Fable 5 is meant to open much of that power to a wider audience while leaving out the most sensitive parts. Anthropic’s goal is to give users the benefit of its smartest model without exposing capabilities that could be misused. **Built-in safety measures remain in place** Anthropic says Fable 5 still includes safeguards for risky topics. When the system detects a query that falls into those categories, the request is routed to another model instead. The fallback model is Claude Opus 4.8, which Anthropic describes as its next most capable model. The company says this arrangement helps it launch faster while keeping safety controls active from day one. Those safeguards are intentionally conservative, which means some harmless requests may also be redirected. Even so, Anthropic says the trigger happens in less than 5% of sessions on average, suggesting the interruption should remain limited for most users. The company is also acknowledging a broader risk: even without a security-silo focus, a powerful model like Fable 5 could still be tested by bad actors, including for malware-related development. That is part of why the guardrails are built into the rollout. **What the launch says about the AI market** The release reflects how quickly the AI industry is moving toward broader access to more powerful systems. Models that were once considered too sensitive for general users are increasingly being adapted for everyday products. For Anthropic, Fable 5 is a balancing act between capability and restraint. It expands access to the company’s smartest AI while keeping a clear line around the areas that carry the most risk. For subscribers on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans, the immediate benefit is obvious during the promotional window. They can use Mythos-grade intelligence without paying beyond their existing s...

LLMs.title: NVIDIA GB300 Dominates Agentic AI Workloads With 20x Performance Leap Over Hopper As Rubin Nears Launch - Wccftech
LLMs.titleZh: NVIDIA GB300 Dominates Agentic AI Workloads With 20x Performance Leap Over Hopper As Rubin Nears Launch - Wccftech
LLMs.content: [AI](https://wccftech.com/topic/ai/) [Hardware](https://wccftech.com/topic/hardware/) # NVIDIA GB300 Dominates Agentic AI Workloads With 20x Performance Leap Over Hopper As Rubin Nears Launch [Hassan Mujtaba](https://wccftech.com/author/hms/) • [Add Wccftech on Google](https://google.com/preferences/source?q=wccftech.com) [Comments](#comments "View comments") NVIDIA's [Blackwell GB300](https://wccftech.com/nvidias-blackwell-ultra-pushes-agentic-ai-performance-to-new-heights/) has posted record performance in AA-AgentPerf, a new benchmark that measures Agentic AI workflows. ## NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GB300 is 20 Times Faster Than Hopper In Agentic AI, Records Highest Performance In Latest Benchmarks Artificial Analysis has a new benchmark out called [AA-AgentPerf](https://artificialanalysis.ai/methodology/agentperf), which measures how many active agents an inference deployment can support under realistic workloads, which include: [##### **Related Story** NVIDIA’s First Co-Packaged Optics Switch Lands at Lambda, Cutting 3kW Per Rack and Freeing Power for 3,137 Extra GPUs](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-first-co-packaged-optics-switch-lands-at-lambda-cutting-3kw-per-rack/) * **Real agentic trajectories** — multi-turn coding sessions with interleaved reasoning, tool calls, and variable context lengths (not synthetic uniform prompts). * **Sustained concurrent load** — simulated agents maintain continuous in-flight requests, stressing KV cache reuse, speculative decoding, and scheduler behavior. * **Market-derived SLO tiers** — performance thresholds based on Artificial Analysis serverless API benchmarking data, reflecting quality-of-service levels observed across providers. * **Continuously updated** — results are updated on an ongoing basis as new hardware, software stacks, and model versions become available. * **Production-ready** — models are tested with realistic optimizations enabled and production-scale deployment topologies. The AA-AgentPerf benchmark is used to measure three key metrics, which form the basis of modern-day AI deployments, such as: * **Time to First Token (TTFT):** Per-request latency from sending the request to receiving the first output token. * **Output Speed:** Per-request output tokens per second, measured after the first token is received. * **System Output Throughput:** Aggregate output tokens per second across all concurrent agents. NVIDIA is now [publishing its first benchmarks](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-achieves-leading-agentic-coding-performance-on-first-agentic-ai-benchmark/) in AgentPerf measures using DeepSeek V4 Pro on its GB300 NVL72 platform. This model represents the type of Frontier models that power agents today & are widely used for AI. In the first round of benchmarks, NVIDIA has recorded the fastest performance with its GB300 hardware, posting a 20x lead (per MegaWatt) over its older HGX H200 platform. GB300 can sustain up to 60,000 concurrent agents per MW, a massive leap over Hopper. | | | | | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **Benchmark** | **Value of metric** | **NVIDIA GB300****NVL72** | **NVIDIA H200** | | Concurrent agents per MW | **Energy efficiency**: How many active agents a system can support for a given power budget | 61.4K | 2.6K | | Concurrent agents per GPU | **Hardware efficiency**: How much serving capacity is achieved per GPU | 57.5 | 1.4 | NVIDIA states that the performance highlights NVIIA's GB300 NVL72 and Blackwell's ability to run large-scale agentic coding workloads while keeping the GPUs fully utilized across several concurrent agent sessions. Looking forward, [NVIDIA's Rubin](https://wccftech.com/nvidia-vera-rubin-enters-full-production-ready-to-power-agentic-ai-factories/) is just on the horizon and is expected to extend these leads through a supercharged AI architecture, which will offer 50 PFLOPs of compute from NVFP4, and with the Vera CPU, the LLM tool calls and end-to-end performance will see major performance and efficiency gains. **About the [a...

LLMs.title: A novel belief-degree-based uncertain Tchebycheff norm DEA model for the case study of risks prioritizing in e-business projects - Nature
LLMs.titleZh: A novel belief-degree-based uncertain Tchebycheff norm DEA model for the case study of risks prioritizing in e-business projects - Nature
LLMs.content: [Skip to main content](#content) [Download PDF](/articles/s41598-026-48479-2.pdf) * Article * [Open access](https://www.springernature.com/gp/open-science/about/the-fundamentals-of-open-access-and-open-research) * Published: # A novel belief-degree-based uncertain Tchebycheff norm DEA model for the case study of risks prioritizing in e-business projects * [Masoomeh Shahbazi](#auth-Masoomeh-Shahbazi-Aff1)[1](#Aff1), * [Hamidreza Taghizadeh Rajaei](#auth-Hamidreza_Taghizadeh-Rajaei-Aff1)[1](#Aff1), * [Kazem Askarifar](#auth-Kazem-Askarifar-Aff1)[1](#Aff1), * [Ali Mahmoodirad](#auth-Ali-Mahmoodirad-Aff2)[2](#Aff2) & * … * [Sadegh Niroomand](#auth-Sadegh-Niroomand-Aff3)[3](#Aff3) [*Scientific Reports*](/srep) **volume 16**, Article number: 18499 (2026) [Cite this article](#citeas) ## Abstract E-business projects may face several risks that are potential to affect the success of such projects. As an important issue for a project manager in this field, the potential risks should be prioritized according to their potential impact on the success of the project. In this study, the potential risks of the e-business sector are to be evaluated and ranked based on the success criteria of such projects. For this aim a complete set of risks compared to the literature is considered and their impact on the project success criteria are determined linguistically by the experts of the field as a case study. To respect the uncertain nature of the evaluations, the linguistic evaluations are converted to belief-degree-based uncertain values. Then for the first time, a data envelopment analysis (DEA) model is used to evaluate and rank such risks. For this aim, the classical Tchebycheff norm DEA model is extended to a belief-degree-based uncertain environment for the first time. An extensive computational study including sensitivity analysis and comparative study is performed by the uncertain Tchebycheff norm model to evaluate and rank the risks of the case study. Based on the obtained results “introducing new technology” and “cultural risk” are the most and least important risks respectively. ## Introduction ### Conceptual background E-business, or even called electronic business, is a type of business to perform commercial activities and transactions over the internet. It consists of a wide range of online operations such as online retail, electronic payments, digital marketing, etc. Bai et al.[1](/articles/s41598-026-48479-2#ref-CR1 "Bai, B., Yin, Y. & Gao, J. Designing a critical success factors model for Chinese self-run e-business from resource orchestration theory. J. Manag. Anal. https://doi.org/10.1080/23270012.2024.2439785 (2025)."), Dogan[2](/articles/s41598-026-48479-2#ref-CR2 "Dogan, O. & Gurcan, O. F. Enhancing e-business communication with a hybrid rule-based and extractive-based chatbot. J. Theor. Appl. Electron. Commer. Res. 19(3), 1984–1999 (2024)."). E-business has transformed classical business methods by enabling companies to easily access global markets, decrease operational costs, and increase customer experiences[3](/articles/s41598-026-48479-2#ref-CR3 "Routaib, H., Seddik, S., Elmounadi, A. & El Haddadi, A. Enhancing e-business in industry 4.0: Integrating fog/edge computing with Data LakeHouse for IIoT. Future Gener. Comput. Syst. 166, 107653 (2025)."). In existence of digital platforms, businesses can simplify processes, increase efficiency, and provide personalized services. E-business also provides challenges like cybersecurity threats, data privacy problems, and the need for continuous technological conformity. Effective and good e-business strategies need robust infrastructure, secure payment methods and systems, and a strong online presence to make trust with customers[4](/articles/s41598-026-48479-2#ref-CR4 "Huo, D., Ouyang, R., Tang, A., Gu, W. & Liu, Z. New growth in cross-border e-business: Evidence from gray forecasting to cross-border e-business in China. J. Internet Digit. Econ. 4(1), 12–29 (2024)."). As the digi...

AI Product.titleZh: Experian and ServiceNow Launch 'Agent Operating System' to Bring Regulated Agentic AI to Lending Workflows - The Fintech Times
AI Product.content: [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/thefintechtimes "Facebook")[Twitter](https://twitter.com/thefintechtimes "Twitter")[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/the_fintech_times/ "Instagram")[Linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/10067847/ "Linkedin")[Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCV68tm1NgG-PZzgRmCK5gBA/ "Youtube") * [About](https://thefintechtimes.com/about/) * [Editorial Calendar](https://thefintechtimes.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/2026-Editorial-Calendar-Jan-Dec.pdf) * [Contact Us](https://thefintechtimes.com/contact-us/) * [Advertise](https://thefintechtimes.com/mediapack/) * [Jobs](https://jobs.thefintechtimes.com/) * [Home](https://thefintechtimes.com) * [World-Region-Country](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/) * [Europe](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/europe/) * [Experian and ServiceNow Launch ‘Agent Operating System’ to Bring Regulated Agentic AI to Lending Workflows](https://thefintechtimes.com/experian-and-servicenow-launch-agent-operating-system-to-bring-regulated-agentic-ai-to-lending-workflows/) [AI](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/news/ai/) [Europe](https://thefintechtimes.com/category/world-region-country/europe/) # Experian and ServiceNow Launch ‘Agent Operating System’ to Bring Regulated Agentic AI to Lending Workflows by [The Fintech Times](https://thefintechtimes.com/author/the-fintech-times/) #### *In one of the most significant artificial intelligence infrastructure rollouts at this year’s Money20/20 Europe conference, global information services giant **Experian** has launched the Experian Agent Operating System™ (AOS).* Unveiled on the Horizon Stage in Amsterdam, the platform arrives as a new core capability within Experian’s established Ascend Platform. To accelerate enterprise deployment, software heavyweight **ServiceNow** has signed on as the foundational launch partner under a multi-year global agreement, embedding Experian’s underlying data and risk governance frameworks directly into core corporate workflows. The launch marks a transition for enterprise AI within regulated financial markets, shifting the industry focus away from single-prompt generative chatbots and toward networks of autonomous software agents authorized to execute high-stakes lending decisions. ##### **Dismantling the Enterprise AI Bottleneck** Despite massive industry-wide budgets flowing into artificial intelligence research, financial institutions continue to encounter severe operational bottlenecks when attempting to scale autonomous models. The hurdle rarely stems from the algorithms themselves, but rather from the underlying data management systems. New global research published by Experian alongside the launch highlights these structural vulnerabilities: * **Workflow Integration:** Nearly half of global financial organizations (48 per cent) state that integrating complex data layers into automated AI workflows remains heavily problematic. * **Lineage Obscurity:** Approximately one-third of risk executives report poor or unverified data lineage within their legacy models. * **Siloed Infrastructure:** An additional third identify fragmented data silos across isolated internal teams and vendor systems as a primary roadblock to production. Experian’s Agent Operating System is engineered to bypass these roadblocks. It establishes a unified semantic, trust, and orchestration layer that connects software agents developed by Experian, the financial institutions themselves, and third-party technology providers into one controlled operational ecosystem. ##### **Capitalizing on a Shift in Consumer Trust** The urgency for financial firms to deploy production-grade agentic layers is further reinforced by a rapid evolution in consumer comfort with machine autonomy. The company announced a roughly $10.2 million Series A led by Avenue Growth Partners to scale product development and go-to-market efforts. The funding will support hiring across engineering, product and customer success, as well as continued R&D to improve platform accuracy and expand capabilities beyond core timekeeping workflows. Serving more than 200 law firms, particularly midmarket firms, Billables AI aims to turn operational data into tools for pricing and cost analysis, staffing insights and AI governance. For legal buyers, the platform promises more accurate time capture, greater operational visibility and efficiency gains as firms move away from manual time-entry processes toward data-driven workflows. ## Trend Themes 1. **AI-assisted Timekeeping** — Legal teams gain more accurate billable-hour capture through platforms that infer work activity from integrated business systems without relying on invasive screen monitoring. 2. **Privacy-first Productivity Analytics** — API-based activity intelligence creates room for workplace software that balances operational visibility with stronger employee privacy and data governance standards. 3. **Legal Operations Intelligence** — Operational data from daily legal workflows is becoming a foundation for pricing analysis, staffing decisions and profitability insights across midmarket firms. ## Industry Implications 1. **Legal Technology** — AI-native platforms are reshaping law firm infrastructure by replacing manual administrative processes with automated, data-driven workflow intelligence. 2. **Professional Services** — Billable-work environments benefit from task intelligence systems that connect time capture, cost analysis and resource planning across client-facing teams. 3. **Enterprise Software** — Integrated productivity tools are expanding beyond workflow automation into governance-ready analytics that support compliance, accuracy and privacy-sensitive adoption. AI Product.titleZh: The AI Question BigLaw Doesn’t Want Junior Lawyers Asking - LawFuel
AI Product.content: [Skip to content](#content) # The AI Question BigLaw Doesn’t Want Junior Lawyers Asking [Leave a Comment](https://www.lawfuel.com/the-ai-question-biglaw-doesnt-want-junior-lawyers-asking/#respond) / [Featured](https://www.lawfuel.com/category/featured/), [Law Firms](https://www.lawfuel.com/category/law-firms/), [Law Job News](https://www.lawfuel.com/category/law-jobs/), [Legal AI](https://www.lawfuel.com/category/legal-ai/) / June 14, 2026 / By [LawFuel Contributor](https://www.lawfuel.com/author/lawfuel-contributor/ "View all posts by LawFuel Contributor") Generative AI has arrived inside BigLaw carrying a question that no one is quite ready to answer out loud: if the technology can do what junior associates do, what exactly are junior associates for? It is the most uncomfortable conversation in the legal profession right now and it is only getting louder from what we hear. For decades, major law firms operated on a clear and largely unquestioned model. Junior associates worked punishing hours on research memos, due diligence reviews, contract markups and first drafts. The work was repetitive, often unglamorous, and absolutely central to two things: generating billable revenue and building the technical foundation of a legal career. [Generative AI](https://www.thomsonreuters.com/en-us/posts/legal/generative-ai-in-law/) is now compressing that model in ways the profession is only beginning to reckon with. ## **The Economics Have Already Shifted** Firms are moving fast. Platforms built on systems from [Harvey AI](https://www.harvey.ai), [OpenAI](https://openai.com), [Thomson Reuters](https://www.thomsonreuters.com/en-us/posts/legal/ai-assistant-for-lawyers/) and [LexisNexis](https://www.lexisnexis.com/en-us/products/lexis-plus-ai.page) are being integrated across practices ranging from M&A to litigation support. The pitch to clients is straightforward: faster output, lower cost, greater efficiency. The pitch to associates is more complicated however. A fifth-year associate equipped with AI-assisted drafting tools can, in some practice contexts, produce work that previously required two or three juniors working across several days, which is already occurring across transactional and research-heavy practices. When leverage changes, staffing models are going to follow. That is the part making the associate class uneasy, and understandably so. ## **The Training Problem No One Has Solved** Here is the tension that sits at the centre of this debate: the repetitive work that junior lawyers find least satisfying is often the same work that builds real legal ability. For instance, research memos teach issue spotting and due diligence teaches pattern recognition. First draft submissions also teach how arguments are structured, where they break down, and what a partner actually means when they say “tighten this up.” If all this is handed to AI before a junior lawyer has engaged with the underlying substance then law firms risks producing lawyers who can supervise technology before they have mastered the work itself. Several prominent [legal education commentators](https://law.stanford.edu/codex-the-center-for-legal-informatics/) and professional responsibility scholars have flagged this directly. The [American Bar Association](https://www.americanbar.org/groups/professional_responsibility/publications/model_rules_of_professional_conduct/) places competence obligations squarely on the supervising lawyer — not on the tool. If the supervising lawyer’s foundational skills were themselves built on AI output rather than deep engagement, competence becomes a cascading problem. ## **What Firms Are Doing** Law firm managing partners publicly maintain that AI will “augment rather than replace” lawyers — and for now, that framing holds. No credible analysis suggests mass associate redundancy in the near term. But the profession is already seeing quiet structural changes: * Reduced headcount growth at some firms, particularly at the junior end * Incr...

AI Product.titleZh: SIJE Unveils Agentic AI and 470,000-Class Ontology Supply Chain Platform at VivaTech 2026, Redefining Global Apparel OEM Industry Standards - 에이빙
AI Product.content: # SIJE Unveils Agentic AI and 470,000-Class Ontology Supply Chain Platform at VivaTech 2026, Redefining Global Apparel OEM Industry Standards 2026-06-15     DK Kim Reporter South Korean vertical AI deep-tech pioneer SIJE (CEO Injun Shin) will make its formal European debut at the 10th-anniversary Viva Technology 2026 (VivaTech) in Paris. Founded in 2021 by an apparel industrial engineering expert, the venture capital-backed enterprise will showcase its vertically integrated supply chain management platform. The technology establishes an automated, end-to-end data pipeline that runs directly from Southeast Asian factory sewing floors to international brand headquarters, targeting structural inefficiencies in the $2 trillion global garment manufacturing market. The company will exhibit at the Stylish Korea Pavilion with support from the Korea Institute of Design Promotion (KIDP). Traditional software frameworks, such as generic ERP and PLM systems, are structurally limited to single-country or isolated factory footprints. They routinely fail to unify the complex “three-way trade structure” that connects Western fashion brands, intermediate buying vendors, and remote assembly facilities. SIJE resolves this data fragmentation by constructing the apparel industry’s first Manufacturing Knowledge Graph, comprising over 470,000 process classes. By mapping worker skill matrices, fabric physical attributes, and machine behaviors into a 16-dimensional vector space, the system digitizes legacy human expertise into measurable data assets, enabling manufacturers to predict production timelines with greater accuracy before a single stitch is sewn. SIJE will present its synchronized, three-part data architecture to eliminate information blackouts that divide global fashion networks. The baseline layer is Monolog, an IoT edge device mounted directly onto industrial sewing machines. Deploying a sensor-fusion system that combines vibration accelerometers, pedal pressure gauges, and machine-vision cameras, it automatically monitors 800 to 1,500 work cycles per operator per day. Using advanced Interquartile Range (IQR) outlier removal and time-decay algorithms, it converts raw mechanical vibrations into Standard Minute Value (SMV) tracking with precise ±3%~±5% accuracy. The middle tier is Monolis, a cloud-based integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS) web application. Honored with Germany’s prestigious iF Design Award 2024, Monolis continuously ingests raw telemetry streams from Monolog, as well as from localized factory ERP and MES databases. This creates a clean, real-time data link that allows remote executives to monitor daily output and achievement metrics. The intelligence layer is Tense AI, an autonomous agentic platform built on the LangGraph framework. It deploys seven specialized, collaborative AI agents that cover ordering, production, quality, materials, delivery, forecasting, and validation, enabling automatic calculation of workflow dependencies. When an international buyer issues a sudden Change Order—modifying quantities, colorways, or shipping dates—Tense AI instantly backtracks the entire supply chain from yarn procurement to customs shipment, and outputs optimized structural alternatives. To ensure safe shop-floor deployment, it enforces a 5-level Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) autonomy matrix, dynamically separating auto-execution and manual-approval tracks based on fiscal impact. SIJE’s enterprise-grade reliability has been thoroughly validated through premier global tier-one references. The startup completed a high-level technical Proof of Concept (PoC) rollout across two large-scale manufacturing facilities in Nam Dinh, Vietnam, for Youngone Corporation, a global apparel manufacturing leader. Concurrently, it secured a comprehensive ERP/MES integration deployment contract with YIC, yielding steady Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR). Open Source AI.title: I stopped using cloud image editors after I found this self-hosted alternative - MakeUseOf
Open Source AI.titleZh: I stopped using cloud image editors after I found this self-hosted alternative - MakeUseOf
Open Source AI.content: Sign in now # I stopped using cloud image editors after I found this self-hosted alternative By  [Yadullah Abidi](/author/yadullah-abidi/) Yadullah Abidi is a Computer Science graduate from the University of Delhi and holds a postgraduate degree in Journalism from the Asian College of Journalism, Chennai. With over a decade of experience in Windows and Linux systems, programming, PC hardware, cybersecurity, malware analysis, and gaming, he combines deep technical knowledge with strong editorial instincts. Yadullah currently writes for MakeUseOf as a Staff Writer, covering cybersecurity, gaming, and consumer tech. He formerly worked as Associate Editor at [Candid.Technology](https://candid.technology/author/yadullah/) and as News Editor at [The Mac Observer](https://www.macobserver.com/author/yadullahabidi/), where he reported on everything from raging cyberattacks to the latest in Apple tech. In addition to his journalism work, Yadullah is a full-stack developer with experience in JavaScript/TypeScript, Next.js, the MERN stack, Python, C/C++, and AI/ML. Whether he's analyzing malware, reviewing hardware, or building tools on GitHub, he brings a hands-on, developer’s perspective to tech journalism. Sign in to your MakeUseOf account If you've spent any time managing images and don't have a suite of professional software (or the hardware to run it) ready to go, making changes can get hectic. You use one web tool for compression, another for removing the background, and another to convert formats, and hope for the best when it comes to each tool's data retention policies. Now there are [command-line tools that can edit images faster than any GUIs](https://www.makeuseof.com/command-line-tool-edits-faster-than-gui/) I've used, but they require learning commands. Nothing too difficult, but not everyone's cup of tea either. Well, if you've got a home server running, this Docker container might just be the answer to all your image management problems. ## The self-hosted image editor nobody talks about ### What SnapOtter is and why it replaced cloud tools for me SnapOtter is an open-source, self-hosted image toolkit that packages essentially everything you'd need for image processing into one container. We're talking resize, crop, compress, convert, watermark, color adjustments, GIF tools, collage builders, meme generators, duplicate finders, passport photo generators, and the list goes on. You name a task, and SnapOtter likely has a tool for it. The project is licensed under AGPLv3, which means it's free to self-host forever. It's built as a TypeScript monorepo with a Python layer for the AI features, and the entire stack compiles into a single Docker image. No Redis, no Postgres, no external services to babysit — just a single handy Docker container quietly humming away on your home server. ##### SnapOtter **OS** : Web-based **Developer** : snapotter-hq **Price model** : Free, paid option's available SnapOtter is an open-source, self-hosted image editing and processing platform with 50+ tools, local AI features, pipelines, and a REST API that runs entirely on your own hardware. [See at SnapOtter](https://snapotter.com/) [See at GitHub](https://github.com/snapotter-hq/snapotter) ## You'll have it running faster than expected ### Deployment that doesn't require hours of troubleshooting The quick start command for SnapOtter is literally one line: ``` docker run -d --name snapotter -p 1349:1349 -v snapotter-data:/data snapotter/snapotter:latest ``` That's it. Once the image pulls and the container starts, you can open your browser to http://localhost:1349 or your home server's local IP address, followed by the 1349 port number if you're accessing it from another machine, and you're off to the races. You'll be asked to log in with the default credentials, change your password, and the entire tool library will immediately be available via a clean web UI.
